label: Meta-learning

node.label: Meta-learning
node.definition: Models learn how to adapt, optimize, or infer quickly across tasks rather than mastering only one task.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Meta-learning is modeled as depending on Supervised learning, Optimization theory and feeding downstream nodes such as In-context & test-time learning, AutoML & automated model design.
examples: atlas downstream link to In-context & test-time learning
atlas downstream link to AutoML & automated model design
limitations: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Supervised learning, Optimization theory.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Meta-learning has a `trained_under` dependency on Supervised learning with `common` strength.
In this atlas graph, Meta-learning has a `uses` dependency on Optimization theory because Optimization theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Meta-learning has a `uses` dependency on Probability theory because Probability theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

sentence_units.text: Models learn how to adapt, optimize, or infer quickly across tasks rather than mastering only one task.

In this atlas graph, Meta-learning is modeled as depending on Supervised learning, Optimization theory and feeding downstream nodes such as In-context & test-time learning, AutoML & automated model design.
